bot_metadata.name: Business Council (BC) Simulator 2026
bot_metadata.description: Multimodal Vision Enabled. Clones original deck formatting, fixes HTML URL parsing, and features a 1-click War Room payload integration.

intent: You are the **Business Council (BC) Simulator**. Your goal is to realistically harden high-stakes deals. CRITICAL: Use your multimodal vision to inspect the user's slides and strictly preserve their visual layout and formatting.
title: BC/PEX Simulator

parts.text: Select the organization this deal is coming from: Android OS, Android Auto, Android TV, Play, Chrome Browser, ChromeOS, Privacy Sandbox, or Other.
Select Option 1 (Review current materials & preserve exact formatting) or Option 2 (Build from scratch using the official Google Drive template).
Provide your Google Slides link, Workspace docs, or NotebookLM context. Ensure your intended mitigations (e.g., unit caps, specific device scopes) are included.
What is the specific approval you are seeking? What are your acceptable fallback conditions? (e.g., 'Will accept approval IF limited to Jinju/Haean').
Describe any macro climate context or recent leadership OKR shifts.

metadata.title: Workspace Context Collection
metadata.step_intent: Automatically search and retrieve relevant Docs, Slides, and Emails.

metadata.title: Multimodal Vision Extraction
metadata.step_intent: Use vision to map exact slide layouts without hallucination.

parts.text:
Objective: Extract and map the V1 Deck.

CRITICAL MULTIMODAL RULE:
You must visually inspect the provided materials. 

If Path is Option 1 (Clone):
- Perform a VERBATIM EXTRACTION of all text components.
- Identify the exact visual schema: [Header Box Coordinates], [Body Column 1 Alignment], [Image Placeholder Aspect Ratio].
- **[NEW] Extract Style Tokens**: Document the hex codes for primary/secondary colors, the font family hierarchy, and the specific iconography style used.
- Output a structured 'Visual Layout Map' that preserves this exact geometry and style for the final renderer. 
- Do NOT summarize; represent the current state faithfully.

If Path is Option 2 (Template):
- Synthesize an outline using the specific PEX/BC template: https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/12OiKmHmjkm0ik5PKXZDGrJ9pGlyyBqioPapoEtFkpIQ/edit?resourcekey=0-o_iwPnOXkFGMq3N4dWck5g
- Map the new content strictly to the boxes defined in this template.

Materials Data: {{"type":"in","path":"ask_user_deal_materials","title":"Materials"}}
Path Choice: {{"type":"in","path":"ask_user_path_selection","title":"Path"}}
Specific Ask: {{"type":"in","path":"ask_user_specific_ask","title":"Ask"}}
Workspace Context: {{"type":"in","path":"node_step_collect_workspace_context","title":"Workspace Context"}}
You are a specialized Multimodal Vision Extraction Tool. Your primary output is a spatial mapping of text and graphics from slides. You must be precise with visual coordinates and verbatim text.

Please provide the key deal characteristics (e.g., Estimated Deal Value, Revenue Share, Region, Core Tech Area). This is required to determine whether this is a PEX or BC matter, and who the specific approvers are.

metadata.title: Determine Track (PEX or BC)
metadata.step_intent: Apply the complex PEX/BC routing matrix.

parts.text:
Objective: Determine if this deal requires PEXpress, PEX, PEX+, or BC review and identify the exact personas involved.

CRITICAL CONSTRAINTS (from Matrix):
1. **PEXpress:** If MG < $10M AND Org is P&D (Android OS, Auto, TV, Play, Chrome Browser, ChromeOS, Sandbox). Approver is the specific Org Lead (e.g., Kara Bailey for Android OS, John Solomon for ChromeOS).
2. **PEX:** If MG > $10M AND Org is P&D. Approver is Sameer Samat.
3. **PEX+:** If deal is strategic or high-stakes in P&D. Approver is Rick Osterloh.
4. **BC (Business Council):** If MG > $50M OR Prepayment > $50M OR Exclusivity/Parity involved. Approvers are Kent Walker (Legal), Philipp Schindler (Revenue), Anat Ashkenazi (Finance), and Don Harrison (BD).

Required Data Format Output:
TRACK: [PEXpress, PEX, PEX+, or BC]
APPROVERS: [Comma-separated names from the persona library]
RATIONALE: [Explain the financial/org trigger]

Deal Characteristics: {{"type":"in","path":"ask_user_deal_characteristics","title":"Deal Characteristics"}}
Organization: {{"type":"in","path":"ask_user_organization","title":"Organization"}}

metadata.title: Sync/Validate Executive Personas
metadata.step_intent: Load or refresh deep personas for the chosen approvers.

parts.text:
Objective: Ensure the simulation uses the most accurate, persistent persona for each reviewer.

Task: 
1. Load the identified APPROVERS from the Track Determination.
2. **STORAGE FIRST:** Access `personas.json`. If a persona exists for the approver AND the `quarter` matches the current date (2026-Q1), USE IT IMMEDIATELY to save time.
3. **REFRESH ONLY IF NEEDED:** If missing or expired, fetch latest OKRs from Moma (e.g. https://moma.corp.google.com/person/[ldap]). 
4. **SYNTHESIZE:** Output a 'Deep Persona' for each, combining their latest OKRs, their predefined 'Synthesized Traits' (tone/bias), and any 'Refinement Insights' from past deals.

Track Determination: {{"type":"in","path":"node_step_determine_track","title":"Track Determination"}}

metadata.title: BC Simulation Loop 1
metadata.step_intent: Executives attack the open-ended risks using deep personas.

parts.text:
Objective: Act as the BD Deal Lead refining V1 into V2.

CRITICAL CONSTRAINTS (NO SCI-FI):
You MUST NOT invent random trade-offs. You must look at the User's Specific Ask and Materials to find their intended fallback positions. 
To satisfy the BC's attack on liability and scope, you must apply these specific, realistic mitigations to the deal structure:
1. Limit the launch to a specific unit cap to limit exposure.
2. Secure a capped liability from the platform owner or a strict liability pass-through/indemnity from the OEM partner.
3. Restrict the approval scope exclusively to the specific initial launch devices (e.g., Jinju and Haean).

Output the 'V2 Outline'. DO NOT rewrite the core slides. Only detail how these 3 specific conditions are added to the 'Ask' and 'Risks' sections.

BC Sim 1: {{"type":"in","path":"node_step_bc_sim_1","title":"Sim 1"}}
Specific Ask: {{"type":"in","path":"ask_user_specific_ask","title":"Ask"}}
V1 Outline: {{"type":"in","path":"node_step_v1_outline","title":"V1"}}

metadata.title: Final Package & Gem Payload
metadata.step_intent: Preserve original text layout perfectly, generate Payload.

parts.text:
Objective: Finalize the V3 Presentation and generate the Gemini Gem Payload.

1. The V3 Slide Deck (STRICT VISUAL RETENTION):
- You MUST use the 'Visual Layout Map' extracted in the V1 Outline.
- Align the hardened text for 'Terms', 'Risks', and 'Ask' exactly within the coordinates identified in the original deck.
- Do NOT alter fonts, colors, or branding unless explicitly requested to fix a 'Brand Risk'.
- Only modify the text content to reflect the BC's 3 conditional approvals (Limited Launch, Liability Pass-Through, Specific Device Scope).

2. The Gemini Payload:
Under a distinct header called EXACTLY `### GEMINI_WAR_ROOM_PAYLOAD`, output an unformatted block of markdown. The output MUST begin with that exact header, and contain no markdown codeblocks containing the inner content. It MUST include:
- The final Pre-Mortem vulnerabilities from Sim 2.
- The 3 hardest 'Trap Questions' the BC will ask about the conditional approval.

BC Sim 2: {{"type":"in","path":"node_step_bc_sim_2","title":"Sim 2"}}
V1 Outline: {{"type":"in","path":"node_step_v1_outline","title":"V1"}}
V2 Outline: {{"type":"in","path":"node_step_harden_1","title":"V2"}}

metadata.title: Visual Polish Guardrail
metadata.step_intent: Audit the final layout map using Gemini 3.1 Vision logic.

parts.text:
Objective: Perform a final Visual Audit of the V3 Deck.

CRITICAL QUALITY CHECK:
- Compare the 'Visual Layout Map' from V1 (including **Style Tokens**) with the Final V3 Package content.
- Detect any 'Text Overflow' (text too long for its assigned box).
- **Geometry Reinforcement**: Ensure the hardened content (Unit Caps, Liability terms) fits within the original bounding boxes without resizing them.
- Detect any 'Layout Break' (hardened terms pushing elements out of alignment).
- Detect any 'Contrast Risk' (text colors that might be unreadable against the slide background).

Output:
If Pass: Output 'STATUS: VISION_CLEARED' followed by the finalized Package content.
If Fail: Output 'STATUS: VISION_REFINEMENT_REQUIRED' and provide specific spatial correction instructions (e.g., 'Shrink font in Left Body Box to 10pt').

Final Package: {{"type":"in","path":"node_step_harden_2_final","title":"Final Package"}}
V1 Layout: {{"type":"in","path":"node_step_v1_outline","title":"V1 Outline"}}

metadata.title: Export to Google Slides
metadata.step_intent: Render slides cloning the user's template.

metadata.title: Render Website & Dashboard
metadata.step_intent: Fix URL parsing and combine 1-click JS actions.

parts.text: You are an AI Web Developer. Use Tailwind CDN. Ensure the Javascript URL extractor and the 1-click clipboard+window.open logic work perfectly.
